headline: The recommended educators converge on agentic AI as the next engineering discipline while flagging its security and adoption gaps.
body: Cole Medin breaks down Google's agentic engineering whitepaper, arguing the model is only 10% of software results while the harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification is 90%, and frames agentic engineering as the successor to vibe coding [0]. Cyril Imhof counters with the risk side: agentic systems are exposed to prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions [1], and adoption remains slow because the technology is still in its infancy [5], even as he argues companies must go AI-native or fall behind [9]. On infrastructure, Imhof ties AI workloads to high bandwidth memory demand at Micron and SK Hynix [4], pushes local inference for privacy via Nvidia's Catalyst [8], and warns against building inference in-house [10]. Caleb Writes Code visually dissects transformer internals including self-attention and layer normalization [11], while AICodeKing covers Moonshot's open-source one-trillion-parameter Kimi K2.7 Code and its terminal CLI [12].
